_GetRadioSender.description: Get unit from which we want to transmit a radio message. This has to be an aircraft for subtitles to work.

_GetRadioSenderCoord.description: Get unit from which we want to transmit a radio message. This has to be an aircraft or ground unit for subtitles to work.

lid.description: ID for dcs.log.

modulation.description: The radio modulation. Either radio.modulation.AM or radio.modulation.FM.

dt.description: Time interval in seconds for checking the radio queue.

delay.description: Time delay before starting the radio queue.

Tlast.description: Time (abs) when the last transmission finished.

sendercoord.description: Coordinate from where transmissions are broadcasted.

sendername.description: Name of the sending unit or static.

power.description: Power of radio station in Watts. Default 100 W.

checking.description: Scheduler is checking the radio queue.

schedonce.description: Call ScheduleOnce instead of normal scheduler.

New.description: Create a new RADIOSPEECH object for a given radio frequency/modulation.

AddSentenceToSpeech.description: Add Sentence to the Speech collection.

BuildTree.description: Build the tree structure based on the language words, in order to find the correct sentences and to ignore incomprehensible words.

New.description: Create a new MSRS object. Required argument is the frequency and modulation.

SetBackend.description: Set backend to communicate with SRS.

SetBackendGRPC.description: Set DCS-gRPC as backend to communicate with SRS.

SetBackendHound.description: Set Hound-TTS as backend to communicate with SRS.

SetBackendSRSEXE.description: Set `DCS-SR-ExternalAudio.exe` as backend to communicate with SRS.

SetDefaultBackendGRPC.description: Set DCS-gRPC to be the default backend.

SetDefaultBackendHound.description: Set Hound-TTS to be the default backend.

SetPath.description: Set path to SRS install directory. More precisely, path to where the `DCS-SR-ExternalAudio.exe` is located.

SetVoice.description: Set to use a specific voice. Note that this will override any gender and culture settings as a voice already has a certain gender/culture.

SetVoiceProvider.description: Set to use a specific voice for a given provider. Note that this will override any gender and culture settings.

SetVoiceWindows.description: Set to use a specific voice if Microsoft Windows' native TTS is use as provider. Note that this will override any gender and culture settings.

SetVoiceGoogle.description: Set to use a specific voice if Google is use as provider. Note that this will override any gender and culture settings.

SetVoicePiper.description: Set to use a specific voice if Piper is used as provider (only Hound-TTS backend). Note that this will override any gender and culture settings.

SetSpeakerPiper.description: Set to use a specific speaker for a voice if Piper is used as provider (only Hound-TTS backend).

SetVoiceAzure.description: Set to use a specific voice if Microsoft Azure is use as provider (only DCS-gRPC backend). Note that this will override any gender and culture settings.

SetVoiceAmazon.description: Set to use a specific voice if Amazon Web Service is use as provider (only DCS-gRPC backend). Note that this will override any gender and culture settings.

SetCoordinate.description: Set the coordinate from which the transmissions will be broadcasted. Note that this is only a factor if SRS has line-of-sight or distance enabled.

SetGoogle.description: [Deprecated] Use google text-to-speech credentials. Also sets Google as default TTS provider.

SetGoogleAPIKey.description: [Deprecated] Use google text-to-speech set the API key (only for DCS-gRPC).

SetProvider.description: Set provider used to generate text-to-speech.

SetProviderOptionsGoogle.description: Set provider options and credentials for Google Cloud.

SetProviderOptionsAmazon.description: Set provider options and credentials for Amazon Web Service (AWS). Only supported in combination with DCS-gRPC as backend.

SetProviderOptionsAzure.description: Set provider options and credentials for Microsoft Azure. Only supported in combination with DCS-gRPC as backend.

SetTTSProviderAzure.description: Use Microsoft Azure to provide text-to-speech. Only supported if used in combination with DCS-gRPC as backend.

SetTTSProviderAmazon.description: Use Amazon Web Service (AWS) to provide text-to-speech. Only supported if used in combination with DCS-gRPC as backend.

SetTTSProviderPiper.description: Use Piper to provide text-to-speech. Only supported if used in combination with Hound-TTS as backend.

SetTTSProviderKitten.description: Use Kitten to provide text-to-speech. Only supported if used in combination with Hound-TTS as backend.

SetTTSProviderOpenAI.description: Use OpenAI to provide text-to-speech. Only supported if used in combination with Hound-TTS as backend.

Help.description: Print SRS help to DCS log file.

SetAutoTranslate.description: Auto-translate messages on-the-fly with Hound Translate services. Tested with google cloud.

PlaySoundFile.description: Play sound file (ogg or mp3) via SRS.

PlayTextExt.description: Play text message via MSRS with explicitly specified options.

_GetLatLongAlt.description: Get lat, long and alt from coordinate.

_GetCommand.description: Get SRS command to play sound using the `DCS-SR-ExternalAudio.exe`.

_ExecCommand.description: Execute SRS command to play sound using the `DCS-SR-ExternalAudio.exe`.

_DCSgRPCtts.description: Make DCS-gRPC API call to transmit text-to-speech over SRS.

_HoundTestTone.description: Hound Test Tone function, sends a 2-second 440 Hz sine wave tone directly over SRS, bypassing the TTS engine entirely.

_HoundSpeechTime.description: Hound speech time calculator. Use to determine how long it takes to speak something out.

_HoundTranslate.description: Hound text translator. Use to translate a message into another language and hand the translation to a function.

getSpeechTime.description: Function returns estimated speech time in seconds.

lid.description: Class id string for output to DCS log file.

voice.description: Specific voice. Only used if no explicit provider voice specified.

coordinate.description: Coordinate from where the transmission is send.

Label.description: Label showing up on the SRS radio overlay. Default is "ROBOT". No spaces allowed.

ConfigFileName.description: Name of the standard config file.

ConfigFilePath.description: Path to the standard config file.

ConfigLoaded.description: If `true` if config file was loaded.

poptions.description: Provider options. Each element is a data structure of type `MSRS.ProvierOptions`.

provider.description: Provider of TTS (win, gcloud, azure, amazon, ...).

backend.description: Backend used as interface to SRS (MSRS.Backend.SRSEXE, MSRS.Backend.HOUND or MSRS.Backend.GRPC).

UsePowerShell.description: Use PowerShell to execute the command and not cmd.exe

New.description: Create a new MSRSQUEUE object for a given radio frequency/modulation.

AddTransmission.description: Add a transmission to the radio queue.

SetTransmitOnlyWithPlayers.description: Switch to only transmit if there are players on the server.

CalcTransmisstionDuration.description: Calculate total transmission duration of all transmission in the queue.

lid.description: ID for dcs.log.

dt.description: Time interval in seconds for checking the radio queue.

Tlast.description: Time (abs) when the last transmission finished.

checking.description: If `true`, the queue update function is scheduled to be called again.

key.description: Access key (DCS-gRPC with Google, AWS, AZURE as provider).

secret.description: Secret key (DCS-gRPC with AWS as provider)

NewFromVector.description: Create a new NAVFIX class instance from a given VECTOR.

NewFromCoordinate.description: Create a new NAVFIX class instance from a given COORDINATE.

NewFromLLDMS.description: Create a new NAVFIX instance from given latitude and longitude in degrees, minutes and seconds (DMS).

NewFromLLDD.description: Create a new NAVFIX instance from given latitude and longitude in decimal degrees (DD).

NewFromBeacon.description: Create a new NAVFIX class instance from  BEACONS.Beacon data.

SetIntermediateFix.description: Set whether this is the intermediate fix (IF).

SetInitialApproachFix.description: Set whether this is an initial approach fix (IAF).

SetFinalApproachFix.description: Set whether this is the final approach fix (FAF).

SetMissedApproachFix.description: Set whether this is the final approach fix (FAF).

SetAltMandatory.description: Set mandatory altitude (min alt = max alt).

SetSpeedMin.description: Set minimum allowed speed at this fix.

SetSpeedMax.description: Set maximum allowed speed at this fix.

SetSpeedMandatory.description: Set mandatory speed (min speed = max speed) at this fix.

SetCompulsory.description: Set whether this fix is compulsory.

SetFlyOver.description: Set whether this is a fly-over fix fix.

GetAltitude.description: Get the altitude in feet MSL. If min and max altitudes are set, it will return a random altitude between min and max.

GetSpeed.description: Get the speed. If min and max speeds are set, it will return a random speed between min and max.

MarkerShow.description: Add marker the NAVFIX on the F10 map.

MarkerRemove.description: Remove marker of the NAVFIX from the F10 map.

_GetMarkerText.description: Get text displayed in the F10 marker.

isFlyover.description: Is this a flyover fix (`true`) or turning point otherwise.

isFAF.description: Is this a final approach fix.

isIAF.description: Is this an initial approach fix.

NewFromScenery.description: Create a new NAVAID class instance.

SetFrequency.description: Set frequency the beacon transmits on.

NewFromTable.description: Create a new BEACONS class instance from a given table.

NewFromFile.description: Create a new BEACONS class instance from a given file.

GetVec3.description: Get 3D position vector of a specific beacon.

GetCoordinate.description: Get COORDINATE of a specific beacon.

GetClosestBeacon.description: Find closest beacon to a given coordinate.

GetClosestBeacons.description: Find closest beacons to a given coordinate.

GetBeacons.description: Get table of all beacons, optionally of a given type.

CountBeacons.description: Count beacons, optionally of a given type.

MarkerShow.description: Add markers for all beacons on the F10 map. Optionally, only a specific beacon or a certain beacon type can be marked.

MarkerRemove.description: Remove markers of all beacons from the F10 map. Optionally, remove only marker of a specific beacon or a certain beacon type.

_GetMarkerText.description: Get text displayed in the F10 marker.

NewFromTable.description: Create a new RADIOS class instance from a given table.

NewFromFile.description: Create a new RADIOS class instance from a given file.

GetVec3.description: Get 3D position vector of a specific radio.

GetCoordinate.description: Get COORDINATE of a specific radio.

GetClosestRadio.description: Find closest radio to a given coordinate.

GetClosestRadios.description: Find closest radios to a given coordinate.

MarkerShow.description: Add markers for all radios on the F10 map.

MarkerRemove.description: Remove markers of all radios from the F10 map.

_GetMarkerText.description: Get text displayed in the F10 marker.

callsign.description: Callsigns of the radio (usually the airbase name).

NewFromTable.description: Create a new TOWNS class instance from a given table.

NewFromFile.description: Create a new TOWNS class instance from a given file.

GetVec3.description: Get 3D position vector of a specific town.

GetCoordinate.description: Get COORDINATE of a specific town.

GetCoordRoad.description: Get closest road coordinate of a town.

GetCoordRail.description: Get closest rail coordinate of a town.

GetConnectionRoad.description: Get road or rail connection between two towns.

GetClosestTown.description: Find closest town to a given coordinate.

GetClosestTowns.description: Find closest towns to a given coordinate.

GetTowns.description: Get table of all towns, optionally of a given type.

MarkerShow.description: Add markers for all towns on the F10 map.

MarkerRemove.description: Remove markers of all towns from the F10 map.

_GetMarkerText.description: Get text displayed in the F10 marker.

fields.name: BirthPlace
fields.description: The birthplace enumerator is used to define where an aircraft or helicopter has spawned in association with birth events.

fields.name: VolumeType
fields.description: The volumeType enumerator defines the types of 3d geometery used within the [world.searchObjects](https://wiki.hoggitworld.com/view/DCS_func_searchObjects) function.

world.BirthPlace.description: The birthplace enumerator is used to define where an aircraft or helicopter has spawned in association with birth events.

world.VolumeType.description: The volumeType enumerator defines the types of 3d geometery used within the #world.searchObjects function.

fields.name: speedMax0
fields.description: Max speed in meters/second at zero altitude.

fields.name: speedMax10K
fields.description: Max speed in meters/second at 10k altitude.

Vec2.description: Vec2 is a 2D-vector for the ground plane as a reference plane.

Position3.description: Position is a composite structure. It consists of both coordinate vector and orientation matrix. Position3 (also known as "Pos3" for short) is a table that has following format:

fields.name: x
fields.description: Orientation component of vector pointing East.

fields.name: y
fields.description: Orientation component of vector pointing up.

fields.name: z
fields.description: Orientation component of vector pointing North.

Task.description: A task descriptor (internal structure for DCS World). See https://wiki.hoggitworld.com/view/Category:Tasks.

fields.name: flag
fields.description: enum stores weapon flags. Some of them are combination of another flags.

fields.name: GuidanceType
fields.description: enum that stores guidance methods. Available only for guided weapon (Weapon.Category.MISSILE and some Weapon.Category.BOMB).

fields.name: MissileCategory
fields.description: enum that stores missile category. Available only for missiles (Weapon.Category.MISSILE).

Weapon.flag.description: enum stores weapon flags. Some of them are combination of another flags.

fields.name: AnyUnguidedBomb
fields.description: + Penetrator + NapalmBomb + FAEBomb + ClusterBomb + Dispencer + CandleBomb + ParachuteBomb

Weapon.Category.description: Weapon.Category enum that stores weapon categories.

Weapon.GuidanceType.description: Weapon.GuidanceType enum that stores guidance methods. Available only for guided weapon (Weapon.Category.MISSILE and some Weapon.Category.BOMB).

Weapon.MissileCategory.description: Weapon.MissileCategory enum that stores missile category. Available only for missiles (Weapon.Category.MISSILE).

Weapon.WarheadType.description: Weapon.WarheadType enum that stores warhead types.

fields.name: ID
fields.description: Identifier of an airbase. It assigned to an airbase by the Mission Editor automatically. This identifier is used in AI tasks to refer an airbase that exists (spawned and not dead) or not.

fields.name: Category
fields.description: enum contains identifiers of airbase categories.

fields.name: Desc
fields.description: Airbase descriptor. Airdromes are unique and their types are unique, but helipads and ships are not always unique and may have the same type.

Airbase.Category.description: Enum contains identifiers of airbase categories.

Airbase.Desc.description: Airbase descriptor. Airdromes are unique and their types are unique, but helipads and ships are not always unique and may have the same type.

Controller.description: Controller is an object that performs A.I.-tasks. Other words controller is an instance of A.I.. Controller stores current main task, active enroute tasks and behavior options. Controller performs commands. Please, read DCS A-10C GUI Manual EN.pdf chapter "Task Planning for Unit Groups", page 91 to understand A.I. system of DCS:A-10C.

fields.name: Detection
fields.description: Enum contains identifiers of surface types.

fields.name: IRST
fields.description: Infra-red search and track detection. Numeric value 8.

fields.name: RWR
fields.description: Radar Warning Receiver detection. Numeric value 16.

fields.name: distance
fields.description: Distance to the target is known

fields.name: ID
fields.description: Identifier of an unit. It assigned to an unit by the Mission Editor automatically.

Unit.RefuelingSystem.description: Enum that stores aircraft refueling system types.

Unit.OpticType.description: Enum that stores types of optic sensors.

fields.name: speedMax0
fields.description: Distance / Time maximal TAS at ground level

fields.name: speedMax10K
fields.description: Distance / Time maximal TAS at altitude of 10 km

fields.name: riverCrossing
fields.description: can the vehicle cross a rivers

fields.name: detectionDistanceRBM
fields.description: detection distance for RCS=1m^2 in real-beam mapping mode, nil if radar doesn't support surface/land search

fields.name: detectionDistanceHRM
fields.description: detection distance for RCS=1m^2 in high-resolution mapping mode, nil if radar has no HRM

fields.name: detectionDistanceAir
fields.description: detection distance for RCS=1m^2 airborne target, nil if radar doesn't support air search

fields.name: detectionDistanceIdle
fields.description: detection of tail-on target with heat signature = 1 in upper hemisphere, engines are in idle

fields.name: ID
fields.description: Identifier of a group. It is assigned to a group by Mission Editor automatically.

fields.name: Category
fields.description: Enum contains identifiers of group types.

Group.Category.description: Enum contains identifiers of group types.

Event.description: Event structure. Note that present fields depend on type of event.

fields.name: number
fields.description: subPlace Subplace. Unknown and often just 0.

fields.name: groupID
fields.description: Group ID, *e.g.* of group that added mark point.

fields.name: DCS#Vec3
fields.description: pos Position vector, *e.g.* of mark point.
